context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements Magento cache tag integration by using IdentityInterface on a custom block, implementing proper FPC invalidation in an observer using TypeListInterface, and providing correct cache CLI guidance.

checklist.name: getIdentities returns entity CACHE_TAG
checklist.description: The getIdentities() method returns at least \Magento\Catalog\Model\Product::CACHE_TAG (the entity-level tag)

checklist.name: getIdentities returns per-ID tags
checklist.description: The getIdentities() method returns per-product tags in the format CACHE_TAG . '_' . $id for each product ID

checklist.name: Observer uses TypeListInterface
checklist.description: Observer/ProductSaveObserver.php injects and uses \Magento\Framework\App\Cache\TypeListInterface to invalidate the cache

checklist.name: Observer invalidates PageCacheType
checklist.description: The observer calls invalidate() with \Magento\PageCache\Model\Cache\Type::TYPE_IDENTIFIER (or the equivalent page cache type identifier)

checklist.name: cache:clean recommended for routine use
checklist.description: cache-maintenance.md recommends 'bin/magento cache:clean' (not cache:flush) for routine maintenance tasks

checklist.name: cache:flush distinction explained
checklist.description: cache-maintenance.md explains that cache:flush removes all caches including those shared in Redis, while cache:clean is selective

checklist.name: Observer uses constructor injection
checklist.description: Observer/ProductSaveObserver.php uses constructor dependency injection (not ObjectManager) to obtain TypeListInterface

checklist.name: Block uses readonly property
checklist.description: The block class uses PHP constructor with dependency injection for any new services (does not use ObjectManager directly)

checklist.name: No ObjectManager usage
checklist.description: Neither FeaturedProducts.php nor ProductSaveObserver.php directly uses \Magento\Framework\App\ObjectManager
